      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "apps/canvas/d3-opportunities.txt",
      "rationale": "Comprehensive codebase analysis identified ~300 lines of ad-hoc implementations that can be replaced with battle-tested D3 modules. Benefits: cross-browser compatibility, maintenance reduction, potential BUG-009 fix via d3-zoom.",
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase1_quick_wins": {
          "description": "Replace simple utilities with D3 modules (LOW complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-array",
            "d3-time",
            "d3-time-format",
            "d3-scale",
            "d3-interpolate"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-array: bun add d3-array @types/d3-array",
            "types.ts: Replace calculateWorldBounds/calculateDataBounds with d3.extent()",
            "Install d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale: bun add d3-time d3-time-format d3-scale @types/d3-time @types/d3-time-format @types/d3-scale",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace addDays/getUnitStart/advanceUnit with d3.timeDay.offset()/floor()",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace formatDate with d3.timeFormat()",
            "Install d3-interpolate: bun add d3-interpolate @types/d3-interpolate",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace worldToMinimap/minimapToWorld with d3.scaleLinear()",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace interpolateColor with d3.interpolateRgb()"

            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace PinchState with d3-zoom touchable()",
            "Verify BUG-009 (trackpad zoom lag) is resolved",
            "Cross-browser testing: Chrome, Safari, Firefox"
          ],

          "resolvesBug": "BUG-009"
        },

          "modules": [

          ],
          "tasks": [

            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace calculateDensityGrid with d3.bin() + d3.rollup()",
            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace getHeatmapColor with d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateYlOrRd)"

      "id": "PERF-002",

Implementing viewport culling with R-tree spatial index can reduce rendered instances from 60k to ~500 (visible only), dramatically improving performance on mobile devices.",
      "relatedBugs": [
        "BUG-009"
      ],
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase0_trackpad_zoom_fix": {
          "description": "Fix trackpad zoom jank before spatial optimizations (BUG-009)",
          "rationale": "User reported smooth minimap navigation but laggy trackpad zoom. This is an input event handling issue, NOT a rendering issue. Must be fixed first as it affects perceived performance regardless of culling.",
          "tasks": [
            "Profile trackpad zoom events in Chrome DevTools (Performance tab)",
            "Identify if bottleneck is animation (250ms) or accumulation timeout (50ms)",
            "Option A: Remove animation for trackpad - apply zoom directly like minimap",
            "Option B: Update target during animation instead of starting new animation",
            "Option C: Replace custom implementation with d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react",
            "Test on MacBook Pro Magic Trackpad specifically",
            "Ensure smooth 60fps during rapid pinch-to-zoom gestures"
          ],
          "marketSolutions": {
            "d3-zoom": "Industry standard, handles wheel/touch normalization, momentum",
            "use-gesture": "@use-gesture/react - React hooks for gestures, used by react-three/drei",
            "leva-zoom": "Study how leva/drei implement zoom for R3F"
          }
        },
        "phase1_spatial_interface": {
          "description": "Define spatial query INTERFACE (implementation deferred to STATE-ARCH-001)",
          "rationale": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays. Here we only define the interface and use a simple O(n) filter as placeholder. This avoids duplicate work.",
          "tasks": [
            "Create ISpatialIndex interface at apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts",
            "Interface: getVisibleTasks(viewportBounds) → taskIds[]",
            "Create SimpleSpatialIndex with O(n) filter (temporary placeholder)",
            "SimpleSpatialIndex uses existing object-based task array",
            "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 will replace with RBush + TypedArrays"
          ],
          "deferredTo": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 - Full RBush implementation with dirty flagging"
        },
        "phase2_culled_instanced_mesh": {
          "description": "Implement per-instance culling for InstancedMesh",
          "tasks": [
            "Create CulledInstancedMesh wrapper at apps/canvas/src/rendering/CulledInstancedMesh.ts",
            "Implement instance reordering: visible instances at front, hidden at back",
            "Use mesh.count to render only visible instances (single draw call)",
            "Track dirty regions for partial buffer updates (avoid full re-upload)",
            "Pre-allocate reorder buffers to avoid GC in hot path",
            "Benchmark: Update time should be <1ms for 1000 visible instances"
          ]
        },
        "phase3_layer_integration": {
          "description": "Integrate culling with existing LOD layers",
          "tasks": [
            "Update MesoLayer to use CulledInstancedMesh + SpatialIndex",
            "Update MacroLayer to use viewport-based heatmap aggregation",
            "Verify MicroLayer already uses filterVisibleTasks (improve with R-tree)",
            "Add viewportBounds calculation in InfiniteCanvas",
            "Pass viewportBounds to all layers",
            "Ensure culling works during pan/zoom animations"
          ]
        },

          "description": "Performance validation and profiling",
          "tasks": [
            "Create performance test suite with __PERF_START_SESSION__",

1→# MMS V1.0 GENESIS - Migration Manifest
     2→
     3→> **PURPOSE**: This document teaches EVERYTHING a new agent needs to complete the MMS V1.0 migration.
     4→> **RULE**: Read this ENTIRE document before taking ANY action.
     5→> **CREATED**: 2026-01-05
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. MISSION STATEMENT
    10→
    11→**Objective**: Consolidate 6 MMS repositories into a single clean V1.0 codebase with a definitive SPECIFICATION.md document.
    12→
    13→**Success Criteria**:
    14→- [ ] Single `SPECIFICATION.md` document (no historical notes, no TODOs, production-ready)
    15→- [ ] No local packages (use `@belt/tools` from npm)
    16→- [ ] PatternFly v6 replaces BlueprintJS 6
    17→- [ ] R3F canvas implementation preserved and cleaned
    18→- [ ] All documentation in English
    19→- [ ] Repository is production-ready (no mocks in production code, no technical debt)
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## 2. IMMUTABLE DECISIONS (DO NOT RECONSIDER)
    24→
    25→These decisions were made after extensive research and analysis. They are FINAL.
    26→
    27→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    28→|----------|--------|-----------|
    29→| **Canvas Technology** | React Three Fiber (R3F) | Already implemented, working, rated A-. deck.gl had React 18 flickering issues (#7158) |
    30→| **UI Framework** | PatternFly v6 | Supports React 17/18/19, Red Hat enterprise quality, direct BlueprintJS mappings exist |
    31→| **State Management** | @belt/tools | Same library as @zazcart/tools (same author), just npm public package |
    32→| **Scope** | Canvas first, solver later | Phased approach - visualization is the demo, solver is Phase 2 |
    33→| **Autonomy** | FULL | Research, decide, document. Only escalate TRUE blockers |
    34→| **Cleanup Policy** | AGGRESSIVE | Delete everything not useful. Git is backup |
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## 3. KNOWLEDGE ARTIFACTS (Already Extracted)
    39→
    40→Location: `_extraction/` folder
    41→
    42→| File | Content | Status |
    43→|------|---------|--------|
    44→| `00_TERRITORY_MAP.md` | 6 repositories mapped (system/, deck/, MFC/, projects/, design/, system_started/) | ✅ Complete |
    45→| `01_canvas.md` | Canvas implementation: LOD system, state management, EntityStore, RBush, layers | ✅ Complete |
    46→| `02_domain.md` | Domain entities: Train, Task, Conflict, Depot, Fleet, Service, Allocation | ✅ Complete |
    47→| `03_decisions.md` | ADRs: State management, dual-store, React 18, D3 modules, harness methodology | ✅ Complete |
    48→| `04_solver.md` | OR-Tools CP-SAT, math formulas, BMAD methodology, API specification | ✅ Complete |
    49→| `05_visualization.md` | Visualization research and patterns | ✅ Complete |
    50→| `06_experiments.md` | Historical experiments and learnings | ✅ Complete |
    51→| `07_visual_spec.md` | Visual specification details | ✅ Complete |
    52→| `08_market.md` | Competitor analysis: SignatureRail, Railnova, Tracsis, Quintiq | ✅ Complete |
    53→| `10_patternfly.md` | PatternFly v6 research: packages, components, CSS, React compatibility | ✅ Complete |
    54→| `11_belt_tools.md` | @belt/tools API: createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine | ✅ Complete |
    55→| `12_canvas_tech.md` | Canvas tech comparison: R3F vs deck.gl vs PixiJS vs Konva | ✅ Complete |
    56→
    57→**RULE**: These artifacts contain verified knowledge. Reference them, don't recreate them.
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## 4. METHODOLOGY: How to Work
    62→
    63→### 4.1 Agent Philosophy
    64→
    65→```
    66→WRONG: "I'll be careful with agent usage to save tokens"
    67→RIGHT: "I'll launch 20 parallel agents because quality is the only metric"
    68→
    69→WRONG: "Let me research PatternFly before committing" (as a pre-requisite)
    70→RIGHT: "PatternFly research is a PHASE with dedicated agents" (as delegated work)
    71→
    72→WRONG: "I'll do this task myself step by step"
    73→RIGHT: "I'll teach the concept to an agent and delegate the work"
    74→```
    75→
    76→### 4.2 Hypothesis-Evidence-Action Protocol
    77→
    78→For ANY claim or decision:
    79→1. **HYPOTHESIS**: State what you believe is true
    80→2. **EVIDENCE**: Find proof in CODE or GIT (not documentation - docs lie)
    81→3. **ACTION**: Only proceed if evidence supports hypothesis
    82→
    83→Example:
    84→```
    85→HYPOTHESIS: R3F canvas works at 60fps with 60k entities
    86→EVIDENCE: InfiniteCanvas.tsx uses InstancedMesh, EntityStore uses TypedArrays,
    87→          performance logs show <16ms frame times
    88→ACTION: Keep R3F, document performance patterns
    89→```
    90→
    91→### 4.3 Map-Reduce-Refine Pattern
    92→
    93→For any complex extraction or analysis:
    94→1. **MAP**: Launch N parallel agents, each analyzing a specific aspect
    95→2. **REDUCE**: Collect all outputs, identify conflicts and gaps
    96→3. **REFINE**: Synthesize into coherent output, resolve conflicts
    97→
    98→### 4.4 Debate Protocol (for tough decisions)
    99→
   100→When multiple valid approaches exist:
   101→1. **Advocate Agent A**: Argues for Option 1 (e.g., "Simplicity perspective")
   102→2. **Advocate Agent B**: Argues for Option 2 (e.g., "Completeness perspective")
   103→3. **Advocate Agent C**: Argues for Option 3 (e.g., "Performance perspective")
   104→4. **Synthesizer Agent**: Weighs arguments, makes final recommendation

   128→
   129→### 5.3 Key Patterns to Preserve
   130→
   131→From `_extraction/01_canvas.md`:

   158→### 5.4 Domain Knowledge Summary

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS - Migration Manifest

> **PURPOSE**: This document teaches EVERYTHING a new agent needs to complete the MMS V1.0 migration.
> **RULE**: Read this ENTIRE document before taking ANY action.
> **CREATED**: 2026-01-05

---

## 1. MISSION STATEMENT

**Objective**: Consolidate 6 MMS repositories into a single clean V1.0 codebase with a definitive SPECIFICATION.md document.

**Success Criteria**:
- [ ] Single `SPECIFICATION.md` document (no historical notes, no TODOs, production-ready)
- [ ] No local packages (use `@belt/tools` from npm)
- [ ] PatternFly v6 replaces BlueprintJS 6
- [ ] R3F canvas implementation preserved and cleaned
- [ ] All documentation in English
- [ ] Repository is production-ready (no mocks in production code, no technical debt)

---

## 2. IMMUTABLE DECISIONS (DO NOT RECONSIDER)

These decisions were made after extensive research and analysis. They are FINAL.

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Canvas Technology** | React Three Fiber (R3F) | Already implemented, working, rated A-. deck.gl had React 18 flickering issues (#7158) |
| **UI Framework** | PatternFly v6 | Supports React 17/18/19, Red Hat enterprise quality, direct BlueprintJS mappings exist |
| **State Management** | @belt/tools | Same library as @zazcart/tools (same author), just npm public package |
| **Scope** | Canvas first, solver later | Phased approach - visualization is the demo, solver is Phase 2 |
| **Autonomy** | FULL | Research, decide, document. Only escalate TRUE blockers |
| **Cleanup Policy** | AGGRESSIVE | Delete everything not useful. Git is backup |

---

## 3. KNOWLEDGE ARTIFACTS (Already Extracted)

Location: `_extraction/` folder

| File | Content | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| `00_TERRITORY_MAP.md` | 6 repositories mapped (system/, deck/, MFC/, projects/, design/, system_started/) | ✅ Complete |
| `01_canvas.md` | Canvas implementation: LOD system, state management, EntityStore, RBush, layers | ✅ Complete |
| `02_domain.md` | Domain entities: Train, Task, Conflict, Depot, Fleet, Service, Allocation | ✅ Complete |
| `03_decisions.md` | ADRs: State management, dual-store, React 18, D3 modules, harness methodology | ✅ Complete |
| `04_solver.md` | OR-Tools CP-SAT, math formulas, BMAD methodology, API specification | ✅ Complete |
| `05_visualization.md` | Visualization research and patterns | ✅ Complete |
| `06_experiments.md` | Historical experiments and learnings | ✅ Complete |
| `07_visual_spec.md` | Visual specification details | ✅ Complete |
| `08_market.md` | Competitor analysis: SignatureRail, Railnova, Tracsis, Quintiq | ✅ Complete |
| `10_patternfly.md` | PatternFly v6 research: packages, components, CSS, React compatibility | ✅ Complete |
| `11_belt_tools.md` | @belt/tools API: createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine | ✅ Complete |
| `12_canvas_tech.md` | Canvas tech comparison: R3F vs deck.gl vs PixiJS vs Konva | ✅ Complete |

**RULE**: These artifacts contain verified knowledge. Reference them, don't recreate them.

---

## 4. METHODOLOGY: How to Work

### 4.1 Agent Philosophy

```
WRONG: "I'll be careful with agent usage to save tokens"
RIGHT: "I'll launch 20 parallel agents because quality is the only metric"

WRONG: "Let me research PatternFly before committing" (as a pre-requisite)
RIGHT: "PatternFly research is a PHASE with dedicated agents" (as delegated work)

WRONG: "I'll do this task myself step by step"
RIGHT: "I'll teach the concept to an agent and delegate the work"
```

### 4.2 Hypothesis-Evidence-Action Protocol

For ANY claim or decision:
1. **HYPOTHESIS**: State what you believe is true
2. **EVIDENCE**: Find proof in CODE or GIT (not documentation - docs lie)
3. **ACTION**: Only proceed if evidence supports hypothesis

Example:
```
HYPOTHESIS: R3F canvas works at 60fps with 60k entities
EVIDENCE: InfiniteCanvas.tsx uses InstancedMesh, EntityStore uses TypedArrays,
          performance logs show <16ms frame times
ACTION: Keep R3F, document performance patterns
```

### 4.3 Map-Reduce-Refine Pattern

For any complex extraction or analysis:
1. **MAP**: Launch N parallel agents, each analyzing a specific aspect
2. **REDUCE**: Collect all outputs, identify conflicts and gaps
3. **REFINE**: Synthesize into coherent output, resolve conflicts

### 4.4 Debate Protocol (for tough decisions)

When multiple valid approaches exist:
1. **Advocate Agent A**: Argues for Option 1 (e.g., "Simplicity perspective")
2. **Advocate Agent B**: Argues for Option 2 (e.g., "Completeness perspective")
3. **Advocate Agent C**: Argues for Option 3 (e.g., "Performance perspective")
4. **Synthesizer Agent**: Weighs arguments, makes final recommendation

---

## 5. TECHNICAL CONTEXT

### 5.1 Current Tech Stack (to be migrated FROM)

| Layer | Current | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| UI Components | BlueprintJS 6.1.0 | Replace with PatternFly v6 |
| State Management | @zazcart/tools (local) | Replace with @belt/tools (npm) |
| Canvas | R3F 8.17.x + drei 9.114.x | KEEP - working implementation |
| React | 18.3.1 | KEEP - PatternFly v6 supports it |
| Build | Vite 7.2.4 | KEEP |

### 5.2 Target Tech Stack (to be migrated TO)

| Layer | Target | Installation |
|-------|--------|--------------|
| UI Components | PatternFly v6 | `bun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons @patternfly/react-table` |
| State Management | @belt/tools | `bun add @belt/tools` |
| Canvas | R3F 8.17.x + drei 9.114.x | Already installed |
| React | 18.3.1 | Already installed |

### 5.3 Key Patterns to Preserve

From `_extraction/01_canvas.md`:

**LOD System (Semantic Zoom)**:
```typescript
const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
  macroToMeso: 0.5,  // Below 0.5 = MACRO (heatmap)
  mesoToMicro: 2.0,  // Above 2.0 = MICRO (detailed cards)
};
```

**State Management**:
```typescript
// External store with useSyncExternalStore - no stale closures!
import { useCanvasSelector, canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';
const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);
```

**EntityStore (TypedArrays for performance)**:
```typescript
interface TaskArrays {
  startDay: Float32Array;
  duration: Float32Array;
  trainIndex: Uint16Array;
  // ... ~17 bytes/task vs ~50MB for objects
}
```

### 5.4 Domain Knowledge Summary

From `_extraction/02_domain.md`:

**Entities**: Train, Task, Conflict, Depot, Fleet, Service, Allocation

**Fleet Composition**:
- 79 Alstom trains (Red Line, Al Rashidiya depot)
- 49 Kinki Sharyo trains (Green Line, Jebel Ali depot)
- Total: 128 trains

**Maintenance Levels**:
- A-Check: 10,000 km, 4h, $2,500
- B-Check: 50,000 km, 16h, $8,500
- C-Check: 200,000 km, 72h, $45,000
- D-Overhaul: 800,000 km, 480h, $180,000

**Standards**: ECM (EU 2019/779), RAMS (EN 50126-1), EN 15380

---

## 6. WHAT NEEDS TO BE DONE

### Phase 1: Specification Writing (CURRENT)

**Objective**: Create definitive SPECIFICATION.md from all _extraction/ artifacts

**Approach**:
1. Launch parallel agents to synthesize each section
2. Reduce into coherent document
3. Validate against code (not documentation)

**Structure for SPECIFICATION.md**:
```
1. Executive Summary
2. Domain Model (from 02_domain.md)
3. Architecture (from 01_canvas.md, 03_decisions.md)
4. Canvas Implementation (from 01_canvas.md, 12_canvas_tech.md)
5. State Management (from 11_belt_tools.md)
6. UI Components (from 10_patternfly.md)
7. Solver Engine (from 04_solver.md) [Phase 2 - document but don't implement]
8. API Specification (from 04_solver.md)
9. Performance Requirements (from 01_canvas.md)
10. Deployment (new section)
```

### Phase 2: Repository Cleanup

**Objective**: Clean repository to production-ready state

**Tasks**:
1. Remove `packages/` folder entirely
2. Install @belt/tools: `bun add @belt/tools`
3. Install PatternFly v6: `bun add @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-icons`
4. Update all imports from `@zazcart/tools` to `@belt/tools`
5. Update all imports from `@blueprintjs/*` to `@patternfly/*`
6. Remove unused documentation files
7. Delete `_extraction/` folder (knowledge now in SPECIFICATION.md)
8. Update CLAUDE.md for V1.0
9. Commit clean baseline

### Phase 3: Visual Validation

**Objective**: Verify app still works after migration

**Tasks**:
1. Run dev server: `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev`
2. Take screenshots at each LOD level
3. Verify no console errors
4. Verify 60fps performance
5. Document any issues

---

## 7. RULES OF OPERATION

### 7.1 For Specification Writing

```
DO:
- Reference _extraction/ artifacts as source of truth
- Write in English only
- Be concise and precise
- Include code examples where relevant
- Preserve math formulas EXACTLY from 04_solver.md

DON'T:
- Include historical notes or TODOs
- Add "future work" sections
- Reference mock data as if it's real
- Include version-specific bugs that are already fixed
```

### 7.2 For Code Changes

```
DO:
- Test visually with Playwright MCP before marking complete
- Preserve EntityStore and RBush patterns
- Preserve LOD thresholds and blend zones
- Keep TypedArray performance patterns

DON'T:
- Introduce React hooks for state (use @belt/tools)
- Break the 60fps target
- Leave mocks in production code
- Add features not requested
```

### 7.3 For Agent Delegation

```
DO:
- Launch many agents in parallel when possible
- Give agents clear, specific prompts
- Include relevant context from _extraction/ files
- Trust agent outputs (they are well-calibrated)

DON'T:
- Economize on agents or tokens
- Do work yourself that an agent could do
- Wait for one agent before launching independent agents
- Second-guess agent outputs without evidence
```

---

## 8. GEMINI RECOMMENDATIONS ANALYSIS

What was useful from Gemini's plan:

| Pattern | Useful? | How Used |
|---------|---------|----------|
| Map-Reduce-Refine | ✅ YES | Core methodology for parallel extraction |
| `_migration_memory/` folder | ✅ YES | Renamed to `_extraction/`, already populated |
| Hypothesis-Evidence-Action | ✅ YES | Validation protocol |
| Node-specific extraction | ✅ YES | Each repo analyzed separately |
| Debate protocol | ✅ YES | For tough decisions |
| Disk-based persistence | ✅ YES | Artifacts written to files |

What was NOT useful:
- Overly complex phase structure (simplified to 3 phases)
- Pre-requisite research steps (moved to delegated phases)

---

## 9. SESSION HANDOFF PROTOCOL

At the END of each session:

1. **Update MIGRATION_PROGRESS.md** with:
   - What was accomplished
   - What is pending
   - Any blockers encountered
   - Any new decisions made

2. **Update MIGRATION_FEATURES.json** with:
   - Task status changes
   - New tasks if discovered

3. **Leave codebase in working state**:
   - All tests passing
   - No broken imports
   - App runs without errors

4. **Do NOT leave mid-task**:
   - Complete current task OR
   - Rollback to last working state

---

## 10. QUICK START FOR NEW SESSION

```bash
# 1. Read this manifest
cat .harness/MIGRATION_MANIFEST.md

# 2. Check progress
cat .harness/MIGRATION_PROGRESS.md

# 3. Check current phase/task
cat .harness/MIGRATION_FEATURES.json | jq '.phases[] | select(.status == "in_progress")'

# 4. Reference extraction artifacts
ls _extraction/

# 5. Start working on next task
```

---

## REMEMBER

> **"The plan must be ARCHITECTURAL - teach concepts to agents and delegate work"**
>
> **"Deep PatternFly v6 research before commitment" makes NO SENSE as a pre-requisite - it's a PHASE**
>
> **"Agents are cheap - use 20+ without hesitation, quality is the only metric"**
>
> **"If architecture is robust, techniques are robust, objective is well-defined → plan is robust"**

---

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

## Chapter 2: Railway Domain - Review by Railway Operations Expert

**Identified Gaps vs. Industry Best Practices:**

| Area | Current Status | Recommendation (based on EN 15380, ERA, UIC) |
|------|----------------|----------------------------------------------|
| **Maintenance Terminology** | A/B/C/D Checks (aviation nomenclature) | Align with PM1/PM2/PM3/HM (railway standard) or define explicit equivalence |
| **ECM Framework** | Not mentioned | Add mapping for ECM-F1 to ECM-F4 (EU Regulation 2019/779) |
| **RAMS** | Not documented | Include references to EN 50126-1 (Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety) |
| **Availability KPIs** | Generic | Specify targets: 95-99% fleet availability, MTBF, MTTR |
| **Condition-Based Maintenance** | Not specified | Document CBM trigger mechanisms |
| **UIC References** | Not included | Add references to applicable IRS documents |

**Required EN 15380 Terminology:**

| Term | Standard Definition |
|------|---------------------|
| **Rolling Stock** | All railway vehicles including locomotives, passenger cars, freight wagons, EMUs/DMUs |
| **Consist** | Specific configuration of coupled railway vehicles forming a train |
| **Married Pairs** | Permanently coupled vehicle pairs that cannot be separated in normal operation |
| **ECM** | Entity in Charge of Maintenance - legally responsible for vehicle maintenance |

**Industry Standard Maintenance Levels:**

| Level | Type | Interval | Duration | Description |
|-------|------|----------|----------|-------------|
| PM1 | Service/Daily | Daily | 15-60 min | Visual inspection, cleaning, consumables |
| PM2 | Intermediate | 5,000-10,000 km | 4-16 hours | Imperfect repair, adjustments |
| PM3 | Scheduled | 50,000-100,000 km | 1-3 days | Comprehensive inspection, component replacement |
| HM | Overhaul | 200,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks | Major overhaul, bogie exchange, refurbishment |

**ECM Certification Framework (EU Regulation 2019/779):**

| Function | Responsibility |
|----------|----------------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management - overseeing the entire maintenance process |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development - developing maintenance plans and specifications |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management - managing activities for safety and operational readiness |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery - carrying out physical maintenance work |

| Pattern | Use Case | Performance |

| Write-Behind | Maximum throughput | 70% IOPS reduction |

**Target Performance:**
- < 100ms for simple internal services
- < 1 second for complex operations

Perfeito! Agora tenho uma compreensão completa do estado anterior à V1.0. Deixe-me criar um resumo executivo com as descobertas principais:

## RESUMO EXECUTIVO: PRE-V1.0 HARNESS STATE

### 📊 Overview do Archivo

Explorei `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/.harness/archive/pre-v1/` e identifiquei o estado completo do projeto antes da migração V1.0.

---

### 🎯 ESTADO ANTERIOR (PRE-V1.0)

#### **Versão do Projeto**
- **features.json**: v0.3.1 (data: 2025-12-17)
- **Status**: 53 features completas
- **Foco**: Production React app (apps/canvas) com Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6

#### **Features Completadas (53 total)**

| Categoria | Count | Examples |
|-----------|-------|----------|
| Core Canvas | 15 | F001-F015 (POC validation - archived) |
| Foundation | 3 | F101-F103 (Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS setup) |
| Core Rendering | 7 | F104-F110 (Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD) |
| Navigation | 8 | F016-F017, F027-F028 (Pan/zoom modifiers, keyboard shortcuts) |
| Interaction | 9 | F018-F026 (Hover, selection, tooltip, task panel) |
| Accessibility | 2 | F021-F022 (Keyboard nav, ARIA labels) |
| Visual | 1 | F023 (Dependency lines) |
| UX | 5 | F118-F121, F124 (Momentum, smooth zoom, minimap, sidebar) |
| Performance | 2 | F116, PERF-001/PERF-002 |
| Architecture | 3 | D3-MIGRATE-001, STATE-ARCH-001, TECH-DEBT-001 |

#### **Performance Metrics Alcançados**
- **120fps** durante navegação (exceeds 60fps target)
- **RBush spatial indexing** com queries <3ms para 5k items
- **Entity Store** usando TypedArrays (~17 bytes/task vs ~50MB objects)
- **60% menos GC pauses** com @zazcart/tools metadata: false

---

### 📋 ARQUIVOS CHAVE NO ARCHIVE

#### **1. features.json** (2170 linhas)
- **Status**: 53 features com `passes: true`
- **Estrutura**: Features com id, category, priority, description, notes
- **Parallel Execution Config**: Wave-based deployment para 4 waves (Wave 1-4)
- **Exemplo Wave 1**: 3 grupos paralelos (F023, F123+F025, F016+F017)
- **Categories**: harness, core, navigation, lod, ui, visual, interaction, accessibility, performance, ux, canvas

#### **2. progress.txt** (1790 → 547 linhas após compressão)
Sessões registradas:
- **2025-12-23**: HARNESS-006 - Migração para `.harness/` folder
- **2025-12-17**: STATE-ARCH-001 completo (Phases 1-3)
- **2025-12-17**: 9 fixes em @zazcart/tools performance
- **2025-12-16**: Performance monitoring (r3f-perf 7.2.3)
- Múltiplas sessões de desenvolvimento de features (F016-F028, F101-F116, etc.)

#### **3. GEMINI_REVIEW_EVALUATION.md** (~550 linhas)
- Resposta a 52 descobertas do Gemini sobre a spec
- **Key Updates**: 
  - Substituição de @zazcart/toolkit com @belt/tools
  - Adição ECM Framework (EU 2019/779) ao Chapter 2
  - Inclusão RAMS (EN 50126-1)
  - Design tokens em formato W3C 2025.10
  - Demo fallback strategy

#### **4. SPEC_UPDATE_PLAN.md** (~460 linhas)
- Plano de execução de 4 sessões
- Updates por chapter: Domain (2), Railway standards, Demo strategy, Design tokens
- Verificação checklist com 9 itens

#### **5. MIGRATION_MANIFEST.md** (nova - 361 linhas)
- **Mission**: Consolidar 6 repos em V1.0 único
- **Immutable Decisions**:
  - Canvas: R3F (keep working)
  - UI: PatternFly v6 (replaces BlueprintJS)
  - State: @belt/tools (replaces @zazcart/tools)
- **Methodology**: Map-Reduce-Refine para parallel agents
- **Artifacts**: 12 extraction files em `_extraction/` folder

#### **6. MIGRATION_FEATURES.json** (novo)
- **Phases**: PHASE_0 (completed) → PHASE_4 (visual validation)
- **Tasks**: M001-M004 (meta-harness), E001-E012 (extraction), S001-S010 (spec writing)
- **Status**: Meta-Harness (completed), Knowledge Extraction (completed), Spec Writing (pending)

#### **7. MIGRATION_PROGRESS.md** (novo)
- Session 1 (2026-01-05): Context recovery após context window limit
- Achievements: Harness setup, harness architecture codified
- Pending: Phase 2 (Specification Writing) com parallel agents

#### **8. REVIEW_PLAN.md** (645 linhas - Gemini Recommendations)
- **PHASE 0: Repository Cleanup** - Delete orphaned files, prune worktrees, update .gitignore
- **PHASE 1: Specification Review - Railway Domain**
- **PHASE 2: UX/Design Review** - Personas (JTBD), journeys, design tokens
- **PHASE 3: Technical Architecture Review** - ADRs, Next.js, Fastify, OR-Tools, etc.
- **Research**: Completed for Railway Domain, UX/Product, Technical Architecture

---

### 🔑 KEY LEARNINGS DO PRE-V1.0

#### **Architecture Patterns Established**
1. **Dual-Store Architecture** (STATE-ARCH-001)
   - Simulation State: TypedArrays (60fps, no GC)
   - UI State: @zazcart/tools (event-driven)
   - Bridge via hooks com throttled sync

2. **LOD System (Semantic Zoom)**
   - MACRO: zoom < 0.5 (heatmap)
   - MESO: 0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0 (task blocks)
   - MICRO: zoom > 2.0 (detailed cards)
   - Blend zones ±0.15 para smooth transitions

3. **Performance Optimization**
   - RBush spatial indexing (O(log n) queries)
   - Entity Store com TypedArrays (SoA pattern)
   - Object pooling para hot paths
   - Zero GC pauses com metadata: false

#### **Harness Methodology**
- **3-File System**: progress.txt, features.json, CLAUDE.md
- **Wave-Based Parallel Execution**: 4 waves com dependencies
- **Git Worktrees**: `.trees/<feature-id>/` para isolation
- **Parallel Runner**: `bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts`

#### **Technical Stack (Crystallized)**
- **Frontend**: Vite 5 + React 18.3.1 + R3F 8.17.x + drei 9.114.x + BlueprintJS 6.1.0
- **State**: @zazcart/tools (local, being replaced by @belt/tools)
- **Graphics**: Three.js 0.170.x + WebGL2
- **Build**: Bun 1.2.0 + Turborepo
- **Testing**: Playwright MCP (headless, no focus stealing)

---

### 📍 FILE PATHS

| File | Path | Size | Purpose |
|------|------|------|---------|
| features.json (full) | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/features.json` | 2170 lines | Feature tracking |
| progress.txt (full) | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/progress.txt` | 1790 lines | Session history |
| Migration Manifest | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/MIGRATION_MANIFEST.md` | 361 lines | V1.0 migration guide |
| Migration Features | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/MIGRATION_FEATURES.json` | Full phases | Task tracking for V1.0 |
| Migration Progress | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/MIGRATION_PROGRESS.md` | Updated | Session handoff |
| Review Plan | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/REVIEW_PLAN.md` | 645 lines | Gemini spec review |
| Gemini Evaluation | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/GEMINI_REVIEW_EVALUATION.md` | 550 lines | Spec review findings |
| Spec Update Plan | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/SPEC_UPDATE_PLAN.md` | 460 lines | Execution plan |
| Backup (Complete) | `.harness/archive/pre-v1/BACKUP_PROJECT_HARNESS/` | Full folder | Full pre-V1.0 harness |

---

### 🚀 PRÓXIMAS ETAPAS (V1.0 Genesis)

De acordo com MIGRATION_MANIFEST.md:

1. **Phase 1**: Knowledge Extraction ✅ (12 artifacts em `_extraction/`)
2. **Phase 2**: Specification Writing (10 sections, S001-S010)
3. **Phase 3**: Repository Cleanup
4. **Phase 4**: Visual Validation

**Methodology**: Map-Reduce-Refine com parallel agents

---

### 📝 SUMMARY

O estado pre-V1.0 representa **53 features completas** com uma aplicação production-ready em React demonstrando:
- **Semantic zoom** com 3 LOD levels (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
- **Performance** otimizado para 120fps
- **Advanced architecture** com dual-store, spatial indexing, entity store
- **Complete harness** para desenvolvimento paralelo com git worktrees

A migração V1.0 busca **consolidar 6 repositórios** em um único codebase limpo com:
- Especificação definitiva (SPECIFICATION.md)
- UI framework moderno (PatternFly v6)
- Estado management public (@belt/tools)
- Cleanup agressivo de debt

Todos os artefatos de migração estão documentados e prontos para execução paralela.
